title: Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani

seo.title: Classic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of Classic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) with our easy, authentic recipe. Rich, creamy, and bursting with Indian flavors. Perfect for any meal!
h2Headings: Ingredients You Need for Butter Chicken
Pro Tips for the Best Butter Chicken Results
Serving Suggestions for Your Classic Murgh Makhani
Variations and Substitutions for Butter Chicken
seo.imageAlt: A vibrant bowl of classic butter chicken (murgh makhani) garnished with cream and cilantro, served with naan bread.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich, creamy, and authentic flavors of our Classic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) – a true Indian delight!

description: Experience the unparalleled taste of Classic Butter Chicken, a beloved Indian dish featuring succulent pieces of chicken enveloped in a velvety, rich tomato and butter gravy. This Murgh Makhani recipe delivers a harmonious blend of sweet and savory notes, with subtle spices creating a truly comforting and luxurious meal. Perfect for a special occasion or a delightful weeknight dinner.

ingredients.notes: soaked in warm water for 15 mins

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine chicken pieces with yogurt, 1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste, red chili powder, turmeric powder, garam masala, lemon juice, and salt. Mix well to coat all chicken pieces. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or up to 4 hours for best flavor.

instructions.title: Cook the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil and 2 tablespoons of butter in a large pan or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, working in batches if necessary to avoid overcrowding. Cook for 5-7 minutes per side, until browned and cooked through. Remove chicken and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Gravy Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, melt the remaining 1/4 cup of butter over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of ginger-garlic paste and cook for another 1-2 minutes until fragrant.

instructions.title: Simmer the Tomato Mixture
instructions.text: Stir in the crushed tomatoes, sugar, and kasoori methi. Bring the mixture to a simmer, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 15 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the tomatoes to break down. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.

instructions.title: Blend and Refine the Gravy
instructions.text: Drain the soaked cashews and add them to the tomato mixture. Carefully transfer the hot mixture to a blender and blend until completely smooth. You may need to do this in batches. For an extra smooth gravy, pass it through a fine-mesh sieve back into the pan.

instructions.title: Combine and Finish
instructions.text: Return the smooth gravy to the pan. Add the cooked chicken pieces and stir to combine. Pour in the heavy cream and gently simmer for another 5-7 minutes, allowing the chicken to absorb the rich flavors and the gravy to thicken slightly. Adjust salt if needed.

instructions.title: Serve Your Murgh Makhani
instructions.text: Garnish generously with fresh chopped cilantro. Serve hot with naan bread, basmati rice, or roti for a complete and satisfying Indian meal.

tips: For an extra smoky flavor, char the chicken slightly on a grill or under a broiler before adding it to the gravy. Alternatively, use a traditional dhungar method by placing a small, hot charcoal in a bowl of ghee in the finished curry for a few minutes.

Using good quality canned San Marzano tomatoes or fresh, ripe plum tomatoes makes a significant difference to the gravy's depth and vibrant color. Always blend until perfectly smooth for that restaurant-style texture.

Always finish with a generous swirl of fresh heavy cream and a sprinkle of freshly chopped cilantro just before serving. This not only enhances the presentation but also adds a final touch of richness and freshness.
article:
Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani, is more than just a dish; it's a global culinary phenomenon. Hailing from the vibrant kitchens of India, this iconic curry has captured hearts and palates worldwide with its luscious, creamy texture and subtly spiced, tangy tomato gravy. If you've ever dreamt of recreating that restaurant-quality experience in your own home, you're in the right place. This comprehensive guide will walk you through the journey of preparing a classic Butter Chicken, ensuring a rich, authentic flavor that will transport your taste buds straight to Delhi.

## What is Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani)?

Butter Chicken, known in Hindi as Murgh Makhani, literally translates to "chicken with butter." Its origins trace back to the 1950s in Delhi, specifically to the Moti Mahal restaurant, where Kundan Lal Gujral is credited with its invention. The story goes that leftover tandoori chicken pieces were simmered in a rich tomato gravy, creating a dish that quickly became a sensation.

At its core, Butter Chicken features tender pieces of chicken (often marinated and lightly grilled or pan-fried) bathed in a velvety smooth gravy. This gravy is typically made with tomatoes, butter, cream, and a medley of aromatic Indian spices like ginger, garlic, garam masala, and fenugreek leaves (kasuri methi). The distinct balance of sweet, tangy, and mildly spicy flavors, coupled with its unparalleled richness, makes it a comfort food par excellence. It's a staple in Indian restaurants globally and a beloved dish for family gatherings.

## Ingredients You Need for Butter Chicken

Crafting the perfect Butter Chicken requires a careful selection of fresh ingredients. Here’s what you’ll need for both the chicken marinade and the rich, creamy gravy:

**For the Chicken Marinade:**
*   1 kg boneless, skinless chicken (thighs or breast, cut into 1.5-inch pieces)
*   1/2 cup plain yogurt
*   1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
*   1 tsp red chili powder (Kashmiri chili powder for color, less heat)
*   1/2 tsp turmeric powder
*   1 tsp garam masala
*   1/2 tsp salt
*   1 tbsp lemon juice

**For the Murgh Makhani Gravy:**
*   3 tbsp unsalted butter
*   1 tbsp vegetable oil
*   1 large onion, finely chopped
*   1.5 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
*   1 can (400g) crushed tomatoes or 4-5 large ripe tomatoes, pureed
*   1/4 cup cashews (soaked in warm water for 15-20 minutes, then drained)
*   1 tsp red chili powder
*   1 tsp garam masala
*   1/2 tsp turmeric powder
*   1 tsp salt (or to taste)
*   1 tbsp sugar (adjust to balance tanginess)
*   1/2 cup water or chicken broth
*   1/4 cup heavy cream (plus extra for garnish)
*   1 tsp kasuri methi (dried fenugreek leaves), lightly crushed between palms
*   Fresh coriander leaves, chopped (for garnish)

## Step-by-Step Instructions for Murgh Makhani

Follow these steps meticulously for a truly unforgettable Murgh Makhani experience:

**1. Marinate the Chicken:**
In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with all the marinade ingredients. Mix well, ensuring each piece is coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or ideally 2-4 hours for maximum flavor absorption.

**2. Cook the Chicken:**
Heat 1 tbsp butter and 1 tbsp oil in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Once hot, add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer (you may need to do this in batches to avoid overcrowding). Cook for 5-7 minutes on each side, until the chicken is cooked through and has a slight char. Remove the cooked chicken and set aside.

**3. Prepare the Gravy Base:**
In the same pan, add the remaining butter. Once melted, add the finely chopped onion and sauté until translucent and lightly golden, about 5-7 minutes. Add the ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant.

**4. Build the Flavor:**
Stir in the crushed tomatoes (or puree), soaked cashews, red chili powder, garam masala, turmeric powder, and salt. Mix well. Bring to a simmer, then cover and cook for 10-15 minutes over medium-low heat, stirring occasionally, until the tomatoes have softened and the oil begins to separate from the mixture.

**5. Blend and Strain the Gravy:**
Remove the pan from heat and let the mixture cool slightly. Carefully transfer the contents to a blender. Add 1/2 cup of water or chicken broth. Blend until very smooth. For an extra silky texture, pass the blended gravy through a fine-mesh sieve, pressing down with a spoon to extract all the liquid and discard any solids. This step is crucial for classic restaurant-style Butter Chicken.

**6. Finish the Gravy:**
Return the strained gravy to the pan. Bring it to a gentle simmer over medium-low heat. Stir in the sugar (taste and adjust to your preference – it balances the tanginess of the tomatoes), the heavy cream, and the crushed kasuri methi. Simmer for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the gravy to thicken slightly.

**7. Combine and Serve:**
Add the cooked chicken pieces to the simmering gravy. Stir gently to coat the chicken thoroughly. Continue to simmer for another 5 minutes, ensuring the chicken is heated through and absorbs the rich flavors of the gravy. Garnish with fresh chopped coriander and a swirl of extra cream just before serving.

## Pro Tips for the Best Butter Chicken Results

Achieving that perfect Butter Chicken isn't just about following steps; a few pro tips can elevate your dish from good to extraordinary:

*   **Marinate Longer:** The longer the chicken marinates, the more tender and flavorful it will be. Overnight marination is ideal.
*   **Charred Chicken:** If possible, lightly grill or bake your marinated chicken in a tandoor or oven instead of pan-frying. The smoky char adds a fantastic depth of flavor.
*   **Don't Skip the Cashews:** Cashews (or blanched almonds) are vital for the gravy's characteristic richness and thick, creamy texture.
*   **Blend and Strain:** This step is non-negotiable for a truly smooth, restaurant-quality gravy. It removes any fibrous bits from onions and tomatoes.
*   **Kasuri Methi is Key:** Dried fenugreek leaves are crucial for the authentic flavor of Butter Chicken. Lightly toasting them in a dry pan for a few seconds before crushing enhances their aroma.
*   **Balance the Flavors:** Taste your gravy before the final simmer. Adjust sugar for sweetness, salt for seasoning, and a touch more lemon juice if it needs more tang. The perfect Butter Chicken is a harmony of sweet, tangy, and savory.
*   **Good Quality Tomatoes:** Use ripe, red tomatoes or high-quality canned crushed tomatoes for the best flavor and color.

## Serving Suggestions for Your Classic Murgh Makhani

Butter Chicken is a versatile dish that pairs beautifully with a variety of accompaniments. Here are some classic serving suggestions:

*   **Indian Breads:** Warm, fluffy naan (plain, garlic, or butter naan) is the quintessential partner, perfect for scooping up every last drop of the rich gravy. Roti, paratha, or tandoori bread are also excellent choices.
*   **Rice:** Steamed basmati rice, jeera rice (cumin rice), or saffron rice provide a delicate contrast to the robust curry.
*   **Side Dishes:** A simple cucumber raita (yogurt dip), a fresh green salad, or some pickled onions can offer a refreshing counterpoint to the richness of the Murgh Makhani.
*   **Garnish:** Always finish with a sprinkle of fresh coriander leaves and an artistic swirl of heavy cream. For an extra touch of luxury, a tiny knob of butter melting on top is also delightful.

## Variations and Substitutions for Butter Chicken

While the classic recipe is a masterpiece, there are many ways to adapt Butter Chicken to dietary needs or personal preferences:

*   **Vegetarian Options:** Replace chicken with paneer (Indian cottage cheese) for Paneer Makhani, or use firm tofu, chickpeas, or a medley of vegetables like cauliflower and peas for a delicious vegetarian alternative.
*   **Healthier Version:** Reduce the amount of butter and cream. You can use Greek yogurt in the marinade and a lighter cream or even coconut milk in the gravy for a slightly different but still delicious profile. Grilling or baking the chicken also reduces oil content.
*   **Nut-Free:** If you have a nut allergy, you can substitute the cashews with a small boiled potato (blended in for thickness) or 1-2 tablespoons of melon seeds (char magaz), which are commonly used in Indian gravies and provide a similar richness.
*   **Smoky Flavor:** To impart a traditional smoky flavor without a tandoor, heat a small piece of charcoal until red hot. Place it in a small steel bowl within your finished curry, drizzle a teaspoon of ghee over it, and quickly cover the pot for 2-3 minutes.
*   **Spice Level:** Adjust the amount of red chili powder to your liking. For more heat, use regular red chili powder; for color with less heat, opt for Kashmiri red chili powder.

With this detailed guide, you're well on your way to mastering the art of Butter Chicken. This iconic Indian chicken recipe is a testament to the country's rich culinary heritage, offering a symphony of flavors that will delight anyone who tastes it. Enjoy the process of creating this beloved dish, and savor every creamy, buttery bite!
